150: class _ControlRail extends StatelessWidget {
151:   const _ControlRail({
152:     required this.experiments,
153:     required this.activeExperiment,
154:     required this.selectedMaterial,
155:     required this.sunAngle,
156:     required this.onExperimentSelected,
157:     required this.onMaterialSelected,
158:     required this.onSunAngleChanged,
159:   });

161:   final List<Class6Experiment> experiments;
162:   final Class6Experiment activeExperiment;
163:   final String selectedMaterial;
164:   final double sunAngle;
165:   final ValueChanged<Class6Experiment> onExperimentSelected;
166:   final ValueChanged<String> onMaterialSelected;
167:   final ValueChanged<double> onSunAngleChanged;
168: 
169:   String get _observation {
170:     if (activeExperiment.id == 'transparency') {
171:       switch (selectedMaterial) {
172:         case 'glass':
173:           return 'Light passes through completely. Objects are clearly visible.';
174:         case 'paper':
175:           return 'Light passes partially. Objects look blurry and faint.';
176:         case 'wood':
177:           return 'Light is blocked completely. A dark shadow is formed behind it.';
178:         default:
179:           return activeExperiment.observation;
180:       }
181:     } else if (activeExperiment.id == 'shadow') {
182:       if (sunAngle > 80 && sunAngle < 100) {
183:         return 'Noon: The sun is overhead. The shadow is at its shortest!';
184:       } else if (sunAngle < 40 || sunAngle > 140) {
185:         return 'Morning/Evening: The sun is low in the sky. Shadows become very long.';
186:       }
187:       return 'As the sun moves in a straight line, the shadow always stays on the opposite side.';
188:     } else if (activeExperiment.id == 'pinhole') {
189:       return 'Drag the candle up, down, closer, or farther. Straight-line rays cross at the pinhole, so the image flips and changes size.';
190:     } else if (activeExperiment.id == 'refraction') {
191:       return 'Light bends when it enters water, so the underwater part of the pencil appears shifted at the surface.';
192:     }
193:     return activeExperiment.observation;
194:   }
195: 
196:   String get _studentGuide {
197:     switch (activeExperiment.id) {
198:       case 'transparency':
199:         return 'What to do: Choose glass, oiled paper, or wood.\n'
200:             'What to observe: Glass lets most light pass, paper lets some light pass, wood blocks light.\n'
201:             'Key idea: Materials are transparent, translucent, or opaque.';
202:       case 'shadow':
203:         return 'What to do: Move the sun angle from morning to evening.\n'
204:             'What to observe: Shadow is longest in morning/evening and shortest near noon.\n'
205:             'Key idea: Shadow forms opposite to light direction.';
206:       case 'pinhole':
207:         return 'What to do: Drag the candle around.\n'
208:             'What to observe: Image inside the pinhole camera is upside down and changes size.\n'
209:             'Key idea: Light travels in straight lines.';
210:       case 'refraction':
211:         return 'What to do: Observe the pencil at the water surface.\n'
212:             'What to observe: Pencil looks bent or broken at the boundary.\n'
213:             'Key idea: Light bends when it moves from air to water.';
214:       default:
215:         return activeExperiment.observation;
216:     }
217:   }

441: class _StageArea extends StatelessWidget {
442:   const _StageArea({
443:     required this.experiment,
444:     required this.selectedMaterial,
445:     required this.sunAngle,
446:     required this.mousePosition,

449: 
450:   final Class6Experiment experiment;

461:           child: MouseRegion(
462:             onHover: (event) {

499:           ),
500:         ),
501:       ],
502:     );

505:   String _getObservation() {
506:     if (experiment.id == 'transparency') {
507:       switch (selectedMaterial) {
508:         case 'glass':
509:           return 'Light passes through completely. Objects are clearly visible.';
510:         case 'paper':
511:           return 'Light passes partially. Objects look blurry and faint.';
512:         case 'wood':
513:           return 'Light is blocked completely. A dark shadow is formed behind it.';
514:         default:
515:           return experiment.observation;
516:       }
517:     } else if (experiment.id == 'shadow') {
518:       if (sunAngle > 80 && sunAngle < 100) {
519:         return 'Noon: The sun is overhead. The shadow is at its shortest!';
520:       } else if (sunAngle < 40 || sunAngle > 140) {
521:         return 'Morning/Evening: The sun is low in the sky. Shadows become very long.';
522:       }
523:       return 'As the sun moves in a straight line, the shadow always stays on the opposite side.';
524:     } else if (experiment.id == 'pinhole') {
525:       return 'Drag the candle up, down, closer, or farther. Straight-line rays cross at the pinhole, so the image flips and changes size on the screen.';
526:     } else if (experiment.id == 'refraction') {
527:       return 'Light bends when it enters water, so the underwater part of the pencil appears shifted at the surface.';
528:     }
529:     return experiment.observation;
530:   }

572: class _Class6ExperimentPainter extends CustomPainter {
573:   _Class6ExperimentPainter({
574:     required this.experimentId,
575:     required this.selectedMaterial,
576:     required this.sunAngle,
577:     required this.mousePosition,
578:   });
579: 
580:   final String experimentId;
581:   final String selectedMaterial;
582:   final double sunAngle;
583:   final Offset mousePosition;
584: 
585:   @override
586:   void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
587:     switch (experimentId) {
588:       case 'transparency':
589:         _drawTransparencyExperiment(canvas, size);
590:         break;
591:       case 'shadow':
592:         _drawShadowExperiment(canvas, size);
593:         break;
594:       case 'pinhole':
595:         _drawPinholeExperiment(canvas, size);
596:         break;
597:       case 'refraction':
598:         _drawRefractionExperiment(canvas, size);
599:         break;
600:     }
601:   }
602: 
603:   void _drawTransparencyExperiment(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
604:     final rect = Offset.zero & size;
605:     final background = const LinearGradient(
606:       begin: Alignment.topCenter,
607:       end: Alignment.bottomCenter,
608:       colors: [Color(0xFFF8FBFF), Color(0xFFF7F0E6)],
609:     ).createShader(rect);
610:     canvas.drawRect(rect, Paint()..shader = background);
611: 
612:     final torchX = size.width * 0.14;
613:     final torchY = size.height * 0.5;
614:     final objectX = size.width * 0.42;
615: 
616:     canvas.drawRect(
617:       Rect.fromLTWH(torchX - 40.0, torchY - 15.0, 60.0, 30.0),
618:       Paint()..color = const Color(0xFF2D3436),
619:     );
620:     canvas.drawCircle(
621:       Offset(torchX + 20.0, torchY),
622:       15.0,
623:       Paint()..color = const Color(0xFF2D3436),
624:     );
625: 
626:     final lightColor = const Color(0xFFFFF8B8).withValues(alpha: 0.75);
627:     final lightPath = Path()
628:       ..moveTo(torchX + 20.0, torchY)
629:       ..lineTo(objectX, torchY - 60.0)
630:       ..lineTo(objectX, torchY + 60.0)
631:       ..close();
632:     canvas.drawPath(lightPath, Paint()..color = lightColor);
633: 
634:     double opacity = 0.1;
635:     double passThrough = 0.9;
636:     if (selectedMaterial == 'paper') {
637:       opacity = 0.5;
638:       passThrough = 0.3;
639:     } else if (selectedMaterial == 'wood') {
640:       opacity = 1.0;
641:       passThrough = 0.0;
642:     }
643: 
644:     final objectRect = Rect.fromLTWH(
645:       objectX - 20.0,
646:       torchY - 60.0,
647:       40.0,
648:       120.0,
649:     );
650:     canvas.drawRect(
651:       objectRect,
652:       Paint()..color = const Color(0xFF646464).withValues(alpha: opacity),
653:     );
654:     canvas.drawRect(
655:       objectRect,
656:       Paint()
657:         ..color = const Color(0xFF2D3436)
658:         ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke
659:         ..strokeWidth = 2,
660:     );
661: 
662:     if (passThrough > 0) {
663:       final passPath = Path()
664:         ..moveTo(objectX + 20.0, torchY - 60.0)
665:         ..lineTo(size.width, torchY - 100.0)
666:         ..lineTo(size.width, torchY + 100.0)
667:         ..lineTo(objectX + 20.0, torchY + 60.0)
668:         ..close();
669:       canvas.drawPath(
670:         passPath,
671:         Paint()
672:           ..color = const Color(
673:             0xFFFFFFC8,
674:           ).withValues(alpha: passThrough * 0.5),
675:       );
676:     }
677: 
678:     if (passThrough < 0.5) {
679:       final shadowPath = Path()
680:         ..moveTo(objectX + 20.0, torchY - 60.0)
681:         ..lineTo(size.width, torchY - 80.0)
682:         ..lineTo(size.width, torchY + 80.0)
683:         ..lineTo(objectX + 20.0, torchY + 60.0)
684:         ..close();
685:       canvas.drawPath(
686:         shadowPath,
687:         Paint()
688:           ..color = Colors.black.withValues(alpha: (1 - passThrough) * 0.15),
689:       );
690:     }
691: 
692:     final labelStyle = TextPainter(textDirection: TextDirection.ltr);
693:     labelStyle.text = const TextSpan(
694:       text: 'Light Source',
695:       style: TextStyle(
696:         color: Color(0xFF64748B),
697:         fontSize: 12,
698:         fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
699:       ),
700:     );
701:     labelStyle.layout();
702:     labelStyle.paint(canvas, Offset(torchX - 32.0, torchY + 25.0));
703: 
704:     labelStyle.text = TextSpan(
705:       text: selectedMaterial == 'glass'
706:           ? 'Transparent'
707:           : selectedMaterial == 'paper'
708:           ? 'Translucent'
709:           : 'Opaque',
710:       style: const TextStyle(
711:         color: Color(0xFF64748B),
712:         fontSize: 12,
713:         fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
714:       ),
715:     );
716:     labelStyle.layout();
717:     labelStyle.paint(canvas, Offset(objectX - 25.0, torchY + 75.0));
718:   }
719: 
720:   void _drawShadowExperiment(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
721:     final centerX = size.width / 2;
722:     final groundY = size.height - 120.0;
723:     final pillarHeight = 132.0;
724:     final pillarWidth = 34.0;
725:     final pillarX = centerX - pillarWidth / 2;
726: 
727:     final sky = LinearGradient(
728:       begin: Alignment.topCenter,
729:       end: Alignment.bottomCenter,
730:       colors: const [Color(0xFFEEF7FF), Color(0xFFFFF8DA), Color(0xFFF5F0DB)],
731:     ).createShader(Rect.fromLTWH(0, 0, size.width, groundY));
732:     canvas.drawRect(
733:       Rect.fromLTWH(0, 0, size.width, groundY),
734:       Paint()..shader = sky,
735:     );
736: 
737:     final groundGrad =
738:         LinearGradient(
739:           begin: Alignment.topCenter,
740:           end: Alignment.bottomCenter,
741:           colors: const [
742:             Color(0xFFD8D2B8),
743:             Color(0xFFB6C98C),
744:             Color(0xFF89A663),
745:           ],
746:         ).createShader(
747:           Rect.fromLTWH(0, groundY, size.width, size.height - groundY),
748:         );
749:     canvas.drawRect(
750:       Rect.fromLTWH(0, groundY, size.width, size.height - groundY),
751:       Paint()..shader = groundGrad,
752:     );
753: 
754:     final rad = sunAngle * math.pi / 180.0;
755:     final sunRadius = math.min(280.0, size.width * 0.28);
756:     final sunX = centerX + sunRadius * math.cos(math.pi - rad);
757:     final sunY = groundY - sunRadius * math.sin(math.pi - rad);
758: 
759:     final shadowLen = pillarHeight / math.tan(rad);
760:     final shadowOpacity = math.max(
761:       0.06,
762:       0.24 - math.min(shadowLen.abs() / 1200.0, 0.14),

965:   void _drawCandle(Canvas canvas, double cx, double baseY, double scale) {
966:     final bodyW = 20 * scale;
967:     final bodyH = 62 * scale;
968:     final flameH = 26 * scale;
969:     final bodyX = cx - bodyW / 2;

1000:     final flamePath = Path()
1001:       ..moveTo(cx, bodyY - flameH)

1004:         bodyY - 12 * scale,
1005:         cx,
1006:         bodyY + 2 * scale,
1007:       )
1008:       ..quadraticBezierTo(
1009:         cx - 12 * scale,
1010:         bodyY - 12 * scale,
1011:         cx,
1012:         bodyY - flameH,
1013:       );
1014:     canvas.drawPath(flamePath, Paint()..color = const Color(0xFFFFF4B3));
1015:     canvas.drawPath(
1016:       flamePath,
1017:       Paint()
1018:         ..color = const Color(0xFFF1C40F)
1019:         ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke
1020:         ..strokeWidth = 2,
1021:     );

1024:   void _drawProjectedCandle(

1153:     const bottomLength = 128.0;
1154:     final topX = pivotX - math.cos(pencilAngle) * topLength;
1155:     final topY = pivotY - math.sin(pencilAngle) * topLength;

1157:     final rawBottomY = pivotY + math.sin(pencilAngle) * bottomLength;

123:   void _calculatePrism() {
124:     final a = double.tryParse(_prismAController.text.trim());
125:     final d = double.tryParse(_prismDeviationController.text.trim());
126:     if (a == null || d == null) {
127:       setState(() => _inputError = 'Enter valid numbers for A and δ.');
128:       return;
129:     }
130: 
131:     final result = Class11OpticsCalculator.calculatePrism(
132:       apexAngleDeg: a,
133:       deviationAngleDeg: d,
134:     );
135:     setState(() {
136:       _prismResult = result;
137:       _inputError = result.isValid ? null : result.message;
138:     });
139:   }
140: 
141:   void _resetPrism() {
142:     _prismAController.text = '60';
143:     _prismDeviationController.text = '40';
144:     _calculatePrism();
145:   }
146: 
147:   void _calculateLens() {
148:     final u = double.tryParse(_lensObjectDistanceController.text.trim());
149:     final f = double.tryParse(_lensFocalLengthController.text.trim());
150:     if (u == null || f == null) {
151:       setState(
152:         () => _inputError =
153:             'Enter valid numbers for object distance and focal length.',
154:       );
155:       return;
156:     }
157: 
158:     final result = Class11OpticsCalculator.calculateLens(
159:       type: _lensType,
160:       objectDistanceCm: u,
161:       focalLengthCm: f,
162:     );
163:     setState(() {
164:       _lensResult = result;
165:       _inputError = result.isValid ? null : result.message;
166:     });
167:   }
168: 
169:   void _resetLens() {
170:     _lensType = LensType.convex;
171:     _lensObjectDistanceController.text = '60';
172:     _lensFocalLengthController.text = '20';
173:     _calculateLens();
174:   }
175:

202:                         lensObjectDistanceController:

205:                         lensType: _lensType,

212:                         onCalculateTir: _calculateTir,

530:   static String _fmt(double value, {int decimals = 1}) {
531:     if (value.isNaN || value.isInfinite) return '-';
532:     return value.toStringAsFixed(decimals);
533:   }
534: 
535:   static String _tirStateLabel(TirState state) {
536:     switch (state) {
537:       case TirState.refraction:
538:         return 'Refraction';
539:       case TirState.critical:
540:         return 'Critical angle';
541:       case TirState.totalInternalReflection:
542:         return 'Total internal reflection';
543:       case TirState.invalid:
544:         return 'Invalid input';
545:     }
546:   }
547: 
548:   static String _lensNatureLabel(LensImageNature nature) {
549:     switch (nature) {
550:       case LensImageNature.real:
551:         return 'Real';
552:       case LensImageNature.virtual:
553:         return 'Virtual';
554:       case LensImageNature.atInfinity:
555:         return 'At infinity';
556:       case LensImageNature.invalid:
557:         return 'Invalid input';
558:     }
559:   }
560: 
561:   static String _lensOrientationLabel(LensOrientation orientation) {
562:     switch (orientation) {
563:       case LensOrientation.inverted:
564:         return 'Inverted';
565:       case LensOrientation.upright:
566:         return 'Upright';
567:       case LensOrientation.none:
568:         return '-';
569:     }
570:   }
571: 
572:   static String _lensSizeLabel(LensSize size) {
573:     switch (size) {
574:       case LensSize.magnified:
575:         return 'Magnified';
576:       case LensSize.diminished:
577:         return 'Diminished';
578:       case LensSize.sameSize:
579:         return 'Same size';
580:       case LensSize.undefined:
581:         return '-';
582:     }
583:   }
584: }

790:   String _equationText() {
791:     if (experiment.id == 'tir') {
792:       return 'n1 sin i = n2 sin r\nic = sin⁻¹(n2 / n1), n2 = 1 (air)';
793:     }
794:     if (experiment.id == 'prism') {
795:       return 'At minimum deviation:\nn = sin((A + δ)/2) / sin(A/2)';
796:     }
797:     return 'Thin lens formula:\n1/f = 1/v - 1/u\nMagnification m = v/u';
798:   }
799: 
800:   String _readoutText() {
801:     if (experiment.id == 'tir') {
802:       final critical = tirResult.criticalAngleDeg.isNaN
803:           ? '-'
804:           : '${tirResult.criticalAngleDeg.toStringAsFixed(2)}°';
805:       final refracted = tirResult.refractionAngleDeg == null
806:           ? '-'
807:           : '${tirResult.refractionAngleDeg!.toStringAsFixed(2)}°';
808:       return 'i = ${tirResult.incidentAngleDeg.toStringAsFixed(2)}°\nic = $critical\nr = $refracted\nState = ${tirResult.state.name}';
809:     }
810:     if (experiment.id == 'prism') {
811:       final n = prismResult.refractiveIndex.isNaN
812:           ? '-'
813:           : prismResult.refractiveIndex.toStringAsFixed(3);
814:       return 'A = ${prismResult.apexAngleDeg.toStringAsFixed(2)}°\nδ = ${prismResult.deviationAngleDeg.toStringAsFixed(2)}°\nn = $n';
815:     }
816:     final imageDist = lensResult.imageDistanceCm == null
817:         ? '∞'
818:         : '${lensResult.imageDistanceCm!.toStringAsFixed(2)} cm';
819:     final magnification = lensResult.magnification == null
820:         ? '-'
821:         : lensResult.magnification!.toStringAsFixed(2);
822:     return 'Lens = ${lensResult.type.name}\nv = $imageDist\nm = $magnification\nNature = ${lensResult.imageNature.name}';
823:   }
824: 
825:   String _observationText() {
826:     if (experiment.id == 'tir') return tirResult.message;
827:     if (experiment.id == 'prism') return prismResult.message;
828:     return lensResult.message;
829:   }
830: }

870: class _ExperimentPainter extends CustomPainter {
871:   _ExperimentPainter({
872:     required this.experimentId,
873:     required this.tirResult,
874:     required this.prismResult,
875:     required this.lensResult,
876:   });
877: 
878:   final String experimentId;
879:   final TirResult tirResult;
880:   final PrismResult prismResult;
881:   final LensResult lensResult;
882: 
883:   @override
884:   void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
885:     switch (experimentId) {
886:       case 'tir':
887:         _drawTir(canvas, size);
888:         break;
889:       case 'prism':
890:         _drawPrism(canvas, size);
891:         break;
892:       case 'lens':
893:         _drawLens(canvas, size);
894:         break;
895:     }
896:   }
897: 
898:   void _drawTir(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
899:     final rect = Offset.zero & size;
900:     canvas.drawRect(
901:       rect,
902:       Paint()
903:         ..shader = const LinearGradient(
904:           colors: [Color(0xFFF8FBFF), Color(0xFFF7F0E6)],
905:         ).createShader(rect),
906:     );
907: 
908:     final boundaryY = size.height * 0.5;
909:     final hit = Offset(size.width * 0.55, boundaryY);
910: 
911:     canvas.drawRect(
912:       Rect.fromLTWH(0, 0, size.width, boundaryY),
913:       Paint()..color = const Color(0xFFEAF3FF),
914:     );
915:     canvas.drawRect(
916:       Rect.fromLTWH(0, boundaryY, size.width, size.height - boundaryY),
917:       Paint()..color = const Color(0xFF0F766E).withValues(alpha: 0.45),
918:     );
919:     canvas.drawLine(
920:       Offset(0, boundaryY),
921:       Offset(size.width, boundaryY),
922:       Paint()
923:         ..color = Colors.white
924:         ..strokeWidth = 3,
925:     );
926:     canvas.drawLine(
927:       Offset(hit.dx, 0),
928:       Offset(hit.dx, size.height),
929:       Paint()
930:         ..color = const Color(0xFF94A3B8)
931:         ..strokeWidth = 2,
932:     );
933:     _drawLabel(
934:       canvas,
935:       'Normal',
936:       Offset(hit.dx + 8, 18),
937:       color: const Color(0xFF334155),
938:     );
939:     _drawLabel(
940:       canvas,
941:       'Interface',
942:       Offset(14, boundaryY - 24),
943:       color: const Color(0xFF334155),
944:     );
945:     _drawLabel(
946:       canvas,
947:       'Rarer medium (air)',
948:       const Offset(14, 14),
949:       color: const Color(0xFF1E3A8A),
950:     );
951:     _drawLabel(
952:       canvas,
953:       'Denser medium (glass/water)',
954:       Offset(14, boundaryY + 10),
955:       color: const Color(0xFF064E3B),
956:     );
957:     if (tirResult.criticalAngleDeg.isFinite) {
958:       final icRad = tirResult.criticalAngleDeg * math.pi / 180;
959:       final icEnd = Offset(
960:         hit.dx - math.sin(icRad) * 90,
961:         hit.dy + math.cos(icRad) * 90,
962:       );
963:       _drawDashedLine(
964:         canvas,
965:         start: hit,
966:         end: icEnd,
967:         color: const Color(0xFF0F766E),
968:         strokeWidth: 2,
969:       );
970:       _drawLabel(
971:         canvas,
972:         'ic',
973:         Offset(hit.dx - 50, hit.dy + 36),
974:         color: const Color(0xFF0F766E),
975:         fontSize: 13,
976:       );
977:     }
978: 
979:     final iRad = tirResult.incidentAngleDeg * math.pi / 180;
980:     final source = Offset(
981:       hit.dx - math.sin(iRad) * 220,
982:       hit.dy + math.cos(iRad) * 220,
983:     );
984:     canvas.drawLine(
985:       source,
986:       hit,
987:       Paint()
988:         ..color = const Color(0xFFF97316)
989:         ..strokeWidth = 4,
990:     );
991:     _drawLabel(
992:       canvas,
993:       'Incident ray',
994:       Offset((source.dx + hit.dx) / 2 - 70, (source.dy + hit.dy) / 2 + 12),
995:       color: const Color(0xFF9A3412),
996:     );
997: 
998:     final incidentDirection = math.atan2(
999:       source.dy - hit.dy,
1000:       source.dx - hit.dx,
1001:     );
1002:     canvas.drawArc(
1003:       Rect.fromCircle(center: hit, radius: 34),
1004:       math.pi / 2,
1005:       incidentDirection - (math.pi / 2),
1006:       false,
1007:       Paint()
1008:         ..color = const Color(0xFFF97316)
1009:         ..strokeWidth = 2
1010:         ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke,
1011:     );
1012:     _drawLabel(
1013:       canvas,
1014:       'i',
1015:       Offset(hit.dx - 26, hit.dy + 24),
1016:       color: const Color(0xFFF97316),
1017:       fontSize: 13,
1018:     );
1019: 
1020:     if (tirResult.state == TirState.refraction &&
1021:         tirResult.refractionAngleDeg != null) {
1022:       final rRad = tirResult.refractionAngleDeg! * math.pi / 180;
1023:       final refractedEnd = Offset(
1024:         hit.dx + math.sin(rRad) * 220,
1025:         hit.dy - math.cos(rRad) * 220,
1026:       );
1027:       canvas.drawLine(
1028:         hit,
1029:         refractedEnd,
1030:         Paint()
1031:           ..color = const Color(0xFF2563EB)
1032:           ..strokeWidth = 4,
1033:       );
1034:       _drawLabel(
1035:         canvas,
1036:         'Refracted ray',
1037:         Offset(
1038:           (hit.dx + refractedEnd.dx) / 2 - 54,
1039:           (hit.dy + refractedEnd.dy) / 2 - 24,
1040:         ),
1041:         color: const Color(0xFF1D4ED8),
1042:       );
1043:       final refractedDirection = math.atan2(
1044:         refractedEnd.dy - hit.dy,
1045:         refractedEnd.dx - hit.dx,
1046:       );
1047:       canvas.drawArc(
1048:         Rect.fromCircle(center: hit, radius: 34),
1049:         -math.pi / 2,
1050:         refractedDirection - (-math.pi / 2),
1051:         false,
1052:         Paint()
1053:           ..color = const Color(0xFF2563EB)
1054:           ..strokeWidth = 2
1055:           ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke,
1056:       );
1057:       _drawLabel(
1058:         canvas,
1059:         'r',
1060:         Offset(hit.dx + 10, hit.dy - 32),
1061:         color: const Color(0xFF2563EB),
1062:         fontSize: 13,
1063:       );
1064:     }
1065:     if (tirResult.state == TirState.critical) {
1066:       final grazingEnd = Offset(size.width - 20, boundaryY - 1);
1067:       canvas.drawLine(
1068:         hit,
1069:         grazingEnd,
1070:         Paint()
1071:           ..color = const Color(0xFF2563EB)
1072:           ..strokeWidth = 4,
1073:       );
1074:       _drawLabel(
1075:         canvas,
1076:         'Refracted ray (r = 90°)',
1077:         Offset(hit.dx + 24, boundaryY - 24),
1078:         color: const Color(0xFF1D4ED8),
1079:       );
1080:     }
1081:     if (tirResult.state != TirState.refraction) {
1082:       final reflectedEnd = Offset(
1083:         hit.dx + math.sin(iRad) * 220,
1084:         hit.dy + math.cos(iRad) * 220,
1085:       );
1086:       canvas.drawLine(
1087:         hit,
1088:         reflectedEnd,
1089:         Paint()
1090:           ..color = const Color(0xFFFDE047)
1091:           ..strokeWidth = 4,
1092:       );
1093:       _drawLabel(
1094:         canvas,
1095:         'Reflected ray',
1096:         Offset(
1097:           (hit.dx + reflectedEnd.dx) / 2 - 46,
1098:           (hit.dy + reflectedEnd.dy) / 2 - 18,
1099:         ),
1100:         color: const Color(0xFFA16207),
1101:       );
1102:     }
1103:     canvas.drawCircle(hit, 5, Paint()..color = Colors.white);
1104:     _drawLabel(
1105:       canvas,
1106:       'Point of incidence',
1107:       Offset(hit.dx + 8, hit.dy + 6),
1108:       color: const Color(0xFF334155),
1109:     );
1110:   }
1111: 
1112:   void _drawPrism(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
1113:     final rect = Offset.zero & size;
1114:     canvas.drawRect(
1115:       rect,
1116:       Paint()
1117:         ..shader = const LinearGradient(
1118:           colors: [Color(0xFF1A1A2E), Color(0xFF16213E)],
1119:         ).createShader(rect),
1120:     );
1121: 
1122:     final cx = size.width * 0.42;
1123:     final cy = size.height * 0.5;
1124:     final p = math.min(size.width, size.height) * 0.24;
1125: 
1126:     final triangle = Path()
1127:       ..moveTo(cx, cy - p)
1128:       ..lineTo(cx + p * 0.86, cy + p * 0.5)
1129:       ..lineTo(cx - p * 0.86, cy + p * 0.5)
1130:       ..close();
1131: 
1132:     canvas.drawPath(
1133:       triangle,
1134:       Paint()..color = const Color(0xFF0EA5E9).withValues(alpha: 0.22),
1135:     );
1136:     canvas.drawPath(
1137:       triangle,
1138:       Paint()
1139:         ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke
1140:         ..color = const Color(0xFF38BDF8)
1141:         ..strokeWidth = 3,
1142:     );
1143: 
1144:     final entry = Offset(size.width * 0.1, cy);
1145:     final hit = Offset(cx - p * 0.6, cy + p * 0.15);
1146:     final exit = Offset(cx + p * 0.6, cy + p * 0.1);
1147: 
1148:     canvas.drawLine(
1149:       entry,
1150:       hit,
1151:       Paint()
1152:         ..color = Colors.white
1153:         ..strokeWidth = 6
1154:         ..strokeCap = StrokeCap.round,
1155:     );
1156:     canvas.drawLine(
1157:       hit,
1158:       exit,
1159:       Paint()
1160:         ..color = Colors.white70
1161:         ..strokeWidth = 3
1162:         ..strokeCap = StrokeCap.round,
1163:     );
1164: 
1165:     final dispersionFactor = prismResult.refractiveIndex.isFinite
1166:         ? (prismResult.refractiveIndex - 1).clamp(0.2, 0.8)
1167:         : 0.4;
1168:     final colors = <Color>[
1169:       const Color(0xFFFF0000),
1170:       const Color(0xFFFF7F00),
1171:       const Color(0xFFFFFF00),
1172:       const Color(0xFF00FF00),
1173:       const Color(0xFF0000FF),
1174:       const Color(0xFF4B0082),
1175:       const Color(0xFF9400D3),
1176:     ];
1177: 
1178:     for (var i = 0; i < colors.length; i++) {
1179:       final spread = (i - 3) * 0.09 * dispersionFactor;
1180:       final end = Offset(
1181:         size.width * 0.95,
1182:         exit.dy + spread * size.height * 0.35,
1183:       );
1184:       canvas.drawLine(
1185:         exit,
1186:         end,
1187:         Paint()
1188:           ..color = colors[i].withValues(alpha: 0.85)
1189:           ..strokeWidth = 3.5,
1190:       );
1191:     }
1192:   }
1193: 
1194:   void _drawLens(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
1195:     final rect = Offset.zero & size;
1196:     canvas.drawRect(
1197:       rect,
1198:       Paint()
1199:         ..shader = const LinearGradient(
1200:           colors: [Color(0xFFF8FBFF), Color(0xFFEEF2FF)],
1201:         ).createShader(rect),
1202:     );
1203: 
1204:     final cx = size.width * 0.52;
1205:     final cy = size.height * 0.52;
1206:     final focalPx = (lensResult.focalLengthCm * 3).clamp(40, 180);
1207: 
1208:     canvas.drawLine(
1209:       Offset(0, cy),
1210:       Offset(size.width, cy),
1211:       Paint()
1212:         ..color = const Color(0xFF64748B)
1213:         ..strokeWidth = 2,
1214:     );
1215:     _drawLabel(
1216:       canvas,
1217:       'Principal axis',
1218:       Offset(12, cy + 8),
1219:       color: const Color(0xFF334155),
1220:     );
1221:     canvas.drawCircle(
1222:       Offset(cx - focalPx, cy),
1223:       4,
1224:       Paint()..color = const Color(0xFFEF4444),
1225:     );
1226:     canvas.drawCircle(
1227:       Offset(cx + focalPx, cy),
1228:       4,
1229:       Paint()..color = const Color(0xFFEF4444),
1230:     );
1231:     canvas.drawCircle(
1232:       Offset(cx - focalPx * 2, cy),
1233:       3,
1234:       Paint()..color = const Color(0xFFB91C1C),
1235:     );
1236:     canvas.drawCircle(
1237:       Offset(cx + focalPx * 2, cy),
1238:       3,
1239:       Paint()..color = const Color(0xFFB91C1C),
1240:     );
1241:     _drawLabel(
1242:       canvas,
1243:       'F1',
1244:       Offset(cx - focalPx - 18, cy + 10),
1245:       color: const Color(0xFFB91C1C),
1246:     );
1247:     _drawLabel(
1248:       canvas,
1249:       'F2',
1250:       Offset(cx + focalPx + 8, cy + 10),
1251:       color: const Color(0xFFB91C1C),
1252:     );
1253:     _drawLabel(
1254:       canvas,
1255:       '2F1',
1256:       Offset(cx - focalPx * 2 - 20, cy + 10),
1257:       color: const Color(0xFF991B1B),
1258:     );
1259:     _drawLabel(
1260:       canvas,
1261:       '2F2',
1262:       Offset(cx + focalPx * 2 + 8, cy + 10),
1263:       color: const Color(0xFF991B1B),
1264:     );
1265: 
1266:     final lensHeight = size.height * 0.46;
1267:     const lensHalfWidth = 18.0;
1268:     final lensPath = lensResult.type == LensType.convex
1269:         ? (Path()
1270:             ..moveTo(cx, cy - lensHeight / 2)
1271:             ..quadraticBezierTo(cx + lensHalfWidth, cy, cx, cy + lensHeight / 2)
1272:             ..quadraticBezierTo(
1273:               cx - lensHalfWidth,
1274:               cy,
1275:               cx,
1276:               cy - lensHeight / 2,
1277:             ))
1278:         : (Path()
1279:             ..moveTo(cx - lensHalfWidth, cy - lensHeight / 2)
1280:             ..quadraticBezierTo(
1281:               cx - 5,
1282:               cy,
1283:               cx - lensHalfWidth,
1284:               cy + lensHeight / 2,
1285:             )
1286:             ..lineTo(cx + lensHalfWidth, cy + lensHeight / 2)
1287:             ..quadraticBezierTo(
1288:               cx + 5,
1289:               cy,
1290:               cx + lensHalfWidth,
1291:               cy - lensHeight / 2,
1292:             )
1293:             ..close());
1294:     canvas.drawPath(
1295:       lensPath,
1296:       Paint()..color = const Color(0xFF3B82F6).withValues(alpha: 0.7),
1297:     );
1298:     canvas.drawPath(
1299:       lensPath,
1300:       Paint()
1301:         ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke
1302:         ..color = const Color(0xFF1D4ED8)
1303:         ..strokeWidth = 2,
1304:     );
1305:     _drawLabel(
1306:       canvas,
1307:       lensResult.type == LensType.convex ? 'Convex lens' : 'Concave lens',
1308:       Offset(cx - 56, cy - lensHeight / 2 - 22),
1309:       color: const Color(0xFF1E40AF),
1310:       bounds: size,
1311:     );
1312:     canvas.drawCircle(
1313:       Offset(cx, cy),
1314:       3,
1315:       Paint()..color = const Color(0xFF1E40AF),
1316:     );
1317:     _drawLabel(
1318:       canvas,
1319:       'Optical centre (O)',
1320:       Offset(cx + 10, cy - 18),
1321:       color: const Color(0xFF1E40AF),
1322:       bounds: size,
1323:     );
1324: 
1325:     final objectX = (cx - lensResult.objectDistanceCm * 3)
1326:         .clamp(30.0, cx - 20)
1327:         .toDouble();
1328:     const objectHeight = 90.0;
1329:     final objectTip = Offset(objectX, cy - objectHeight);
1330:     _drawArrow(
1331:       canvas,
1332:       base: Offset(objectX, cy),
1333:       tip: objectTip,
1334:       color: const Color(0xFF059669),
1335:     );
1336:     _drawLabel(
1337:       canvas,
1338:       'Object',
1339:       Offset(objectX - 28, objectTip.dy - 22),
1340:       color: const Color(0xFF065F46),
1341:       bounds: size,
1342:     );
1343: 
1344:     if (lensResult.imageDistanceCm != null &&
1345:         lensResult.magnification != null) {
1346:       final imageX = (cx + lensResult.imageDistanceCm! * 3)
1347:           .clamp(20.0, size.width - 20)
1348:           .toDouble();
1349:       final imageHeight = -objectHeight * lensResult.magnification!;
1350:       final imageTip = Offset(imageX, cy + imageHeight);
1351:       _drawArrow(
1352:         canvas,
1353:         base: Offset(imageX, cy),
1354:         tip: imageTip,
1355:         color: const Color(0xFFDC2626),
1356:       );
1357:       _drawLabel(
1358:         canvas,
1359:         lensResult.imageNature == LensImageNature.real
1360:             ? 'Real image'
1361:             : 'Virtual image',
1362:         lensResult.imageNature == LensImageNature.real
1363:             ? Offset(imageX + 8, imageTip.dy - 22)
1364:             : Offset(imageX - 92, imageTip.dy + 10),
1365:         color: const Color(0xFF991B1B),
1366:         bounds: size,
1367:       );
1368: 
1369:       final lensPointParallel = Offset(cx, objectTip.dy);
1370:       final center = Offset(cx, cy);
1371:       final rightEdgeX = size.width - 10;
1372: 
1373:       canvas.drawLine(
1374:         objectTip,
1375:         lensPointParallel,
1376:         Paint()
1377:           ..color = const Color(0xFF16A34A).withValues(alpha: 0.45)
1378:           ..strokeWidth = 2.2,
1379:       );
1380: 
1381:       if (lensResult.type == LensType.convex) {
1382:         final ray1End = _rayEndAtX(
1383:           start: lensPointParallel,
1384:           through: Offset(cx + focalPx, cy),
1385:           targetX: rightEdgeX,
1386:         );
1387:         canvas.drawLine(
1388:           lensPointParallel,
1389:           ray1End,
1390:           Paint()
1391:             ..color = const Color(0xFF16A34A).withValues(alpha: 0.75)
1392:             ..strokeWidth = 2.2,
1393:         );
1394:       } else {
1395:         final ray1End = _rayEndAtX(
1396:           start: lensPointParallel,
1397:           through: Offset(cx + 80, lensPointParallel.dy - 30),
1398:           targetX: rightEdgeX,
1399:         );
1400:         canvas.drawLine(
1401:           lensPointParallel,
1402:           ray1End,
1403:           Paint()
1404:             ..color = const Color(0xFF16A34A).withValues(alpha: 0.75)
1405:             ..strokeWidth = 2.2,
1406:         );
1407:         _drawDashedLine(
1408:           canvas,
1409:           start: lensPointParallel,
1410:           end: imageTip,
1411:           color: const Color(0xFF16A34A),
1412:           strokeWidth: 1.5,
1413:         );
1414:       }
1415:       _drawLabel(
1416:         canvas,
1417:         'Ray 1 (parallel to axis)',
1418:         Offset(cx - 175, objectTip.dy - 30),
1419:         color: const Color(0xFF166534),
1420:         bounds: size,
1421:       );
1422: 
1423:       canvas.drawLine(
1424:         objectTip,
1425:         center,
1426:         Paint()
1427:           ..color = const Color(0xFF2563EB).withValues(alpha: 0.65)
1428:           ..strokeWidth = 2.2,
1429:       );
1430:       final ray2End = _rayEndAtX(
1431:         start: center,
1432:         through: objectTip,
1433:         targetX: rightEdgeX,
1434:       );
1435:       canvas.drawLine(
1436:         center,
1437:         ray2End,
1438:         Paint()
1439:           ..color = const Color(0xFF2563EB).withValues(alpha: 0.65)
1440:           ..strokeWidth = 2.2,
1441:       );
1442:       if (lensResult.imageNature == LensImageNature.virtual) {
1443:         _drawDashedLine(
1444:           canvas,
1445:           start: center,
1446:           end: imageTip,
1447:           color: const Color(0xFF2563EB),
1448:           strokeWidth: 1.5,
1449:         );
1450:       }
1451:       _drawLabel(
1452:         canvas,
1453:         'Ray 2 (through optical centre)',
1454:         Offset(cx - 170, cy + 14),
1455:         color: const Color(0xFF1D4ED8),
1456:         bounds: size,
1457:       );
1458:       if (lensResult.imageNature == LensImageNature.virtual) {
1459:         _drawLabel(
1460:           canvas,
1461:           'Virtual extension',
1462:           Offset(imageX - 84, imageTip.dy + 28),
1463:           color: const Color(0xFF7C3AED),
1464:           bounds: size,
1465:         );
1466:       }
1467:     }
1468:   }
1469: 
1470:   void _drawLabel(
1471:     Canvas canvas,
1472:     String text,
1473:     Offset position, {
1474:     Color color = const Color(0xFF0F172A),
1475:     double fontSize = 11,
1476:     Size? bounds,
1477:   }) {
1478:     final painter = TextPainter(
1479:       text: TextSpan(
1480:         text: text,
1481:         style: TextStyle(
1482:           color: color,
1483:           fontSize: fontSize,
1484:           fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
1485:         ),
1486:       ),
1487:       textDirection: TextDirection.ltr,
1488:     )..layout(maxWidth: 220);
1489:     final drawX = bounds == null
1490:         ? position.dx
1491:         : position.dx.clamp(6.0, bounds.width - painter.width - 6).toDouble();
1492:     final drawY = bounds == null
1493:         ? position.dy
1494:         : position.dy.clamp(6.0, bounds.height - painter.height - 6).toDouble();
1495:     final bgRect = RRect.fromRectAndRadius(
1496:       Rect.fromLTWH(
1497:         drawX - 4,
1498:         drawY - 2,
1499:         painter.width + 8,
1500:         painter.height + 4,
1501:       ),
1502:       const Radius.circular(4),
1503:     );
1504:     canvas.drawRRect(
1505:       bgRect,
1506:       Paint()..color = Colors.white.withValues(alpha: 0.75),
1507:     );
1508:     painter.paint(canvas, Offset(drawX, drawY));
1509:   }
1510: 
1511:   void _drawArrow(
1512:     Canvas canvas, {
1513:     required Offset base,
1514:     required Offset tip,
1515:     required Color color,
1516:   }) {
1517:     final paint = Paint()
1518:       ..color = color
1519:       ..strokeWidth = 3;
1520:     canvas.drawLine(base, tip, paint);
1521:     final headY = tip.dy > base.dy ? -10 : 10;
1522:     canvas.drawLine(Offset(tip.dx - 8, tip.dy + headY), tip, paint);
1523:     canvas.drawLine(Offset(tip.dx + 8, tip.dy + headY), tip, paint);
1524:   }
1525: 
1526:   void _drawDashedLine(
1527:     Canvas canvas, {
1528:     required Offset start,
1529:     required Offset end,
1530:     required Color color,
1531:     double strokeWidth = 2,
1532:     double dashLength = 7,
1533:     double gapLength = 5,
1534:   }) {
1535:     final paint = Paint()
1536:       ..color = color
1537:       ..strokeWidth = strokeWidth;
1538:     final total = (end - start).distance;
1539:     if (total <= 0) return;
1540:     final direction = (end - start) / total;
1541:     var offset = 0.0;
1542:     while (offset < total) {
1543:       final dashStart = start + direction * offset;
1544:       final dashEnd = start + direction * math.min(offset + dashLength, total);
1545:       canvas.drawLine(dashStart, dashEnd, paint);
1546:       offset += dashLength + gapLength;
1547:     }
1548:   }
1549: 
1550:   Offset _rayEndAtX({
1551:     required Offset start,
1552:     required Offset through,
1553:     required double targetX,
1554:   }) {
1555:     final dx = through.dx - start.dx;
1556:     if (dx.abs() < 1e-6) {
1557:       return Offset(targetX, start.dy);
1558:     }
1559:     final slope = (through.dy - start.dy) / dx;
1560:     return Offset(targetX, start.dy + slope * (targetX - start.dx));
1561:   }
1562: 
1563:   @override
1564:   bool shouldRepaint(covariant _ExperimentPainter oldDelegate) {
1565:     return oldDelegate.experimentId != experimentId ||
1566:         oldDelegate.tirResult != tirResult ||
1567:         oldDelegate.prismResult != prismResult ||
1568:         oldDelegate.lensResult != lensResult;
1569:   }

686: class _MirrorPainter extends CustomPainter {
687:   _MirrorPainter(this.result);
688: 
689:   final SphericalMirrorResult result;
690: 
691:   @override
692:   void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
693:     final cy = size.height * 0.62;
694:     final mirrorX = size.width * 0.72;
695:     canvas.drawLine(
696:       Offset(0, cy),
697:       Offset(size.width, cy),
698:       Paint()
699:         ..color = const Color(0xFF94A3B8)
700:         ..strokeWidth = 2,
701:     );
702: 
703:     final mirrorPath = Path();
704:     final mirrorTop = Offset(mirrorX, cy - size.height * 0.33);
705:     final mirrorBottom = Offset(mirrorX, cy + size.height * 0.22);
706:     if (result.type == MirrorType.concave) {
707:       mirrorPath
708:         ..moveTo(mirrorTop.dx, mirrorTop.dy)
709:         ..quadraticBezierTo(
710:           mirrorX + 42,
711:           cy - 12,
712:           mirrorBottom.dx,
713:           mirrorBottom.dy,
714:         );
715:     } else {
716:       mirrorPath
717:         ..moveTo(mirrorTop.dx, mirrorTop.dy)
718:         ..quadraticBezierTo(
719:           mirrorX - 42,
720:           cy - 12,
721:           mirrorBottom.dx,
722:           mirrorBottom.dy,
723:         );
724:     }
725:     canvas.drawPath(
726:       mirrorPath,
727:       Paint()
728:         ..color = const Color(0xFF2563EB)
729:         ..strokeWidth = 4
730:         ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke,
731:     );
732: 
733:     final objectX = (mirrorX - result.objectDistanceCm * 6)
734:         .clamp(30.0, mirrorX - 20)
735:         .toDouble();
736:     final objectBase = Offset(objectX, cy);
737:     final objectTip = Offset(objectX, cy - 80);
738:     _arrow(canvas, objectBase, objectTip, const Color(0xFF059669));
739: 
740:     if (result.imageDistanceCm != null && result.magnification != null) {
741:       final imageX = (mirrorX + result.imageDistanceCm! * 6)
742:           .clamp(20.0, size.width - 20)
743:           .toDouble();
744:       final imageHeight = -80 * result.magnification!;
745:       final imageBase = Offset(imageX, cy);
746:       final imageTip = Offset(imageX, cy - imageHeight);
747:       _arrow(canvas, imageBase, imageTip, const Color(0xFFDC2626));
748:     }
749:   }
750: 
751:   void _arrow(Canvas canvas, Offset base, Offset tip, Color color) {
752:     final paint = Paint()
753:       ..color = color
754:       ..strokeWidth = 3;
755:     canvas.drawLine(base, tip, paint);
756:     final head = tip.dy > base.dy ? -10 : 10;
757:     canvas.drawLine(Offset(tip.dx - 7, tip.dy + head), tip, paint);
758:     canvas.drawLine(Offset(tip.dx + 7, tip.dy + head), tip, paint);
759:   }
760: 
761:   @override
762:   bool shouldRepaint(covariant _MirrorPainter oldDelegate) =>
763:       oldDelegate.result != result;
764: }

996: class _NewtonDiscPainter extends CustomPainter {
997:   @override
998:   void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
999:     final center = Offset(size.width / 2, size.height / 2);
1000:     final radius = size.width / 2;
1001:     final colors = <Color>[
1002:       const Color(0xFFFF0000),
1003:       const Color(0xFFFF7F00),
1004:       const Color(0xFFFFFF00),
1005:       const Color(0xFF00FF00),
1006:       const Color(0xFF0000FF),
1007:       const Color(0xFF4B0082),
1008:       const Color(0xFF9400D3),
1009:     ];
1010: 
1011:     final sweep = (2 * math.pi) / colors.length;
1012:     for (var i = 0; i < colors.length; i++) {
1013:       canvas.drawArc(
1014:         Rect.fromCircle(center: center, radius: radius),
1015:         i * sweep,
1016:         sweep,
1017:         true,
1018:         Paint()..color = colors[i],
1019:       );
1020:     }
1021: 
1022:     canvas.drawCircle(
1023:       center,
1024:       12,
1025:       Paint()
1026:         ..color = const Color(0xFF1E293B)
1027:         ..style = PaintingStyle.fill,
1028:     );
1029:   }
1030: 
1031:   @override
1032:   bool shouldRepaint(covariant CustomPainter oldDelegate) => false;
1033: }

258:     final cx = size.width * 0.4;
259:     final cy = size.height * 0.5;
260:     final mirrorLength = size.width * 0.7;
261:     final rayLength = size.width * 0.3;
262: 
263:     canvas.drawLine(
264:       Offset(cx - mirrorLength / 2, cy),
265:       Offset(cx + mirrorLength / 2, cy),

267:         ..color = const Color(0xFF64748B)
268:         ..strokeWidth = 3,

270: 
271:     canvas.drawLine(
272:       Offset(cx, cy - rayLength),
273:       Offset(cx, cy + rayLength),
274:       Paint()
275:         ..color = const Color(0xFF94A3B8)
276:         ..strokeWidth = 1.5
277:         ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke,
278:     );
279: 
280:     final incidentRad = incidentAngle * math.pi / 180;
281:     final incidentEndX = cx - rayLength * math.sin(incidentRad);
282:     final incidentEndY = cy - rayLength * math.cos(incidentRad);
283: 
284:     canvas.drawLine(
285:       Offset(cx, cy),
286:       Offset(incidentEndX, incidentEndY),
287:       Paint()
288:         ..color = const Color(0xFF059669)

290:     );
291: 
292:     final reflectedEndX = cx + rayLength * math.sin(incidentRad);
293:     final reflectedEndY = cy - rayLength * math.cos(incidentRad);
294: 
295:     canvas.drawLine(
296:       Offset(cx, cy),
297:       Offset(reflectedEndX, reflectedEndY),
298:       Paint()
299:         ..color = const Color(0xFFDC2626)
300:         ..strokeWidth = 3,
301:     );
302: 
303:     final arcRadius = rayLength * 0.4;
304:

307:       ..strokeWidth = 2
308:       ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke;
309: 
310:     canvas.drawArc(
311:       Rect.fromCircle(center: Offset(cx, cy), radius: arcRadius),
312:       -math.pi / 2 - incidentRad,
313:       incidentRad,
314:       false,
315:       arcPaint,
316:     );
317: 
318:     canvas.drawLine(

338:       arcPaint,
339:     );

344:         style: const TextStyle(
345:           color: Color(0xFF059669),
346:           fontSize: 14,
347:           fontWeight: FontWeight.bold,
348:         ),
349:       ),

527: class _KaleidoscopePainter extends CustomPainter {
528:   _KaleidoscopePainter({
529:     required this.mirrorAngleDegrees,
530:     required this.numberOfMirrors,
531:     required this.animationValue,
532:   });
533: 
534:   final double mirrorAngleDegrees;
535:   final int numberOfMirrors;
536:   final double animationValue;
537: 
538:   @override
539:   void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
540:     final center = Offset(size.width / 2, size.height / 2);
541:     final radius = size.width * 0.42;
542: 
543:     canvas.drawCircle(
544:       center,
545:       radius,
546:       Paint()
547:         ..color = const Color(0xFF334155)
548:         ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke
549:         ..strokeWidth = 4,
550:     );
551: 
552:     final colors = [
553:       const Color(0xFFEF4444),
554:       const Color(0xFFF97316),
555:       const Color(0xFFEAB308),
556:       const Color(0xFF22C55E),
557:       const Color(0xFF3B82F6),
558:       const Color(0xFF8B5CF6),
559:     ];
560: 
561:     final angleRad = (mirrorAngleDegrees * math.pi) / 180;
562:     final sectorCount = (2 * math.pi / angleRad).floor();
563: 
564:     if (numberOfMirrors == 2) {
565:       final mirrorLength = radius * 0.85;
566:       final mirror1Angle = -math.pi / 2 - angleRad / 2;
567:       final mirror2Angle = -math.pi / 2 + angleRad / 2;
568: 
569:       canvas.drawLine(
570:         center,
571:         Offset(
572:           center.dx + mirrorLength * math.cos(mirror1Angle),
573:           center.dy + mirrorLength * math.sin(mirror1Angle),
574:         ),
575:         Paint()
576:           ..color = const Color(0xFFCBD5E1)
577:           ..strokeWidth = 4,
578:       );
579:       canvas.drawLine(
580:         center,
581:         Offset(
582:           center.dx + mirrorLength * math.cos(mirror2Angle),
583:           center.dy + mirrorLength * math.sin(mirror2Angle),
584:         ),
585:         Paint()
586:           ..color = const Color(0xFFCBD5E1)
587:           ..strokeWidth = 4,
588:       );
589: 
590:       final objectRadius = radius * 0.6;
591:       final objectAngle = -math.pi / 2 + (animationValue * 2 * math.pi);
592:       final objectX = center.dx + objectRadius * math.cos(objectAngle);
593:       final objectY = center.dy + objectRadius * math.sin(objectAngle);
594: 
595:       canvas.drawCircle(
596:         Offset(objectX, objectY),
597:         8,
598:         Paint()
599:           ..color = const Color(0xFFFBBF24)
600:           ..style = PaintingStyle.fill,
601:       );
602: 
603:       for (var i = 0; i < sectorCount; i++) {
604:         final sectorAngle = i * angleRad;
605:         final innerRadius = radius * 0.15;
606:         final outerRadius = radius * 0.85;
607: 
608:         final path = Path();
609:         final color = colors[i % colors.length];
610: 
611:         for (var j = 0; j <= 8; j++) {
612:           final t = j / 8;
613:           final r = innerRadius + (outerRadius - innerRadius) * t;
614:           final a = sectorAngle + (angleRad * t);
615:           final x = center.dx + r * math.cos(a);
616:           final y = center.dy + r * math.sin(a);
617:           if (j == 0) {
618:             path.moveTo(x, y);
619:           } else {
620:             path.lineTo(x, y);
621:           }
622:         }
623: 
624:         canvas.drawPath(
625:           path,
626:           Paint()
627:             ..color = color.withValues(alpha: 0.7)
628:             ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke
629:             ..strokeWidth = 3,
630:         );
631:       }
632:     } else {
633:       final mirrorLength = radius * 0.85;
634:       final angleBetweenMirrors = (2 * math.pi) / numberOfMirrors;
635: 
636:       for (var i = 0; i < numberOfMirrors; i++) {
637:         final mirrorAngle = (i * angleBetweenMirrors) - math.pi / 2;
638:         final endX = center.dx + mirrorLength * math.cos(mirrorAngle);
639:         final endY = center.dy + mirrorLength * math.sin(mirrorAngle);
640: 
641:         canvas.drawLine(
642:           center,
643:           Offset(endX, endY),
644:           Paint()
645:             ..color = const Color(0xFFCBD5E1)
646:             ..strokeWidth = 4,
647:         );
648:       }
649: 
650:       for (var sector = 0; sector < sectorCount; sector++) {
651:         final sectorAngle = sector * angleRad;
652:         final color = colors[sector % colors.length];
653: 
654:         for (var ring = 1; ring <= 3; ring++) {
655:           final blobRadius = radius * (0.2 + ring * 0.2);
656:           final blobAngle =
657:               sectorAngle +
658:               (ring * 0.3) +
659:               (animationValue * 2 * math.pi / (ring + 1));
660:           final blobX = center.dx + blobRadius * math.cos(blobAngle);
661:           final blobY = center.dy + blobRadius * math.sin(blobAngle);
662: 
663:           canvas.drawCircle(
664:             Offset(blobX, blobY),
665:             6.0 + ring * 2,
666:             Paint()
667:               ..color = color.withValues(alpha: 0.6)
668:               ..style = PaintingStyle.fill,
669:           );
670:         }
671:       }
672:     }
673: 
674:     canvas.drawCircle(
675:       center,
676:       6,
677:       Paint()
678:         ..color = const Color(0xFF1E293B)
679:         ..style = PaintingStyle.fill,
680:     );
681:   }
682: 
683:   @override
684:   bool shouldRepaint(covariant _KaleidoscopePainter oldDelegate) =>
685:       oldDelegate.mirrorAngleDegrees != mirrorAngleDegrees ||
686:       oldDelegate.numberOfMirrors != numberOfMirrors ||
687:       oldDelegate.animationValue != animationValue;
688: }

753:             'Real-world use: This law explains how mirrors form images.',
754:             style: theme.textTheme.bodyMedium,
755:           ),

764:             'What you see: Patterns multiply as angle decreases.\n'

766:             'Formula: Number of reflections = 360° / θ.',

Now I can see the issue. Looking at the screens:

- **Class 6** uses a `Row` layout with `_ControlRail` (352 width) and `_StageArea` (Expanded) in non-compact mode
- **Class 7** uses a `SingleChildScrollView` with centered content constrained to maxWidth: 1180
- **Class 9** uses a `SingleChildScrollView` with centered content constrained to maxWidth: 1180
- **Class 11** uses a `Row` layout with `_ControlRail` (380 width) and `_StageArea` (Expanded) in non-compact mode

Looking at the `_StageArea` in Class 11 (line 731), there are overlay cards positioned at:
1. `top: 24, left: 24, right: compact ? 24 : null` - top-left
2. `top: compact ? 144 : 24, right: 24, left: compact ? 24 : null` - top-right
3. `bottom: 24, left: 24, right: 24` - bottom

In Class 6 `_StageArea` (line 456):
1. `top: 24, left: 24` with `maxWidth: 360` - top-left
2. `left: 24, right: 24, bottom: 24` - bottom

Looking at the `_ExperimentPainter` in Class 11, there's also `_OverlayCard` positioning. The issue is likely that the overlay cards are overlapping with each other or with the custom painted content on smaller screens.

Let me also check the `_StageArea` in Class 6 - looking at lines 479-500:
- Positioned at top: 24, left: 24 - "Quick Fact" card


- Positioned at bottom with left: 24, right: 24 - "Live Observation" card

The problem appears to be that on smaller or medium-sized screens, the control rail takes up significant space while the stage area becomes constrained, causing the overlay cards to stack too closely together or overlap with the canvas content. In Class 11's `_StageArea` (lines 748-784), the cards are positioned with `ConstrainedBox` wrapping, which may not be handling the width constraints properly when the stage area itself is limited.

Looking at the card positioning logic, the first card uses `ConstrainedBox(maxWidth: compact ? 420 : 360)` with `left: 24` and `right: compact ? 24 : null`, meaning on non-compact screens it extends from left: 24 with no right constraint, potentially causing it to overflow. The second card similarly uses `ConstrainedBox(maxWidth: compact ? 420 : 340)` but positions itself at `top: compact ? 144 : 24` and `right: 24`, which creates a different stacking behavior than intended on various screen sizes.

The "Physics" card in Class 11's `_drawTir` function (around lines 933-109) has similar overflow issues with positioning that doesn't properly account for screen width constraints.

Looking at the layout structure, Class 11 uses a Row with a SizedBox(width: 380) for the control rail and Expanded for the stage area. When the screen is compact (width < 1040), the control rail takes flex: 6 and the stage area takes flex: 5 in a Column layout. The overlay cards appear to be overlapping because they're positioned without accounting for the available width in the stage area.

I notice Class 7 and Class 9 both use ConstrainedBox with maxWidth: 1180 and SafeArea wrapping SingleChildScrollView, which handles small screens better. But Class 6 and Class 11 use Row with SizedBox and Expanded, creating different layout behavior on compact screens that leads to the card overlap issue.

The overlap likely stems from how `_StageArea` positions overlays in Class 6 and Class 11. In Class 11's `_StageArea`, the "Physics" card uses a 360 maxWidth constraint, but this doesn't prevent overlap when the stage area itself becomes too narrow on smaller devices. The positioning logic needs adjustment to account for the available space more dynamically.

parts.text:
Now I understand the issue. Let me analyze the layout differences:

**Class 7 and Class 9** (working well):
- Use a single-column layout with `SafeArea` + `SingleChildScrollView` + centered `ConstrainedBox(maxWidth: 1180)`
- Content flows vertically and scrolls
- No overlapping issues

**Class 6 and Class 11** (overlapping):
- Use a `LayoutBuilder` with two modes:
  - **Compact** (< 1040): Uses `Column` with `_ControlRail` (flex: 6) and `_StageArea` (flex: 5)
  - **Non-compact** (>= 1040): Uses `Row` with `_ControlRail` (SizedBox width) and `_StageArea` (Expanded)
- The `_StageArea` has `Positioned` overlay cards

The overlap issue likely occurs in the **compact mode** where:
1. The stage area is only `flex: 5` out of total 11
2. The overlay cards in `_StageArea` overlap with each other or with the CustomPaint content

Looking at the Class 11 `_StageArea`:
```dart
Positioned(
  top: 24,
  left: 24,
  right: compact ? 24 : null,  // <-- on compact: right=24
  child: ConstrainedBox(
    constraints: BoxConstraints(maxWidth: compact ? 420 : 360),  // <-- on compact: maxWidth=420
    child: _OverlayCard(...),
  ),
),
Positioned(
  top: compact ? 144 : 24,  // <-- on compact: top=144
  right: 24,
  left: compact ? 24 : null,  // <-- on compact: left=24
  child: ConstrainedBox(
    constraints: BoxConstraints(maxWidth: compact ? 420 : 340),  // <-- on compact: maxWidth=420
    child: _OverlayCard(...),
  ),
),
Positioned(
  bottom: 24,
  left: 24,
  right: 24,  // <-- extends full width
  child: _OverlayCard(...),
),
```

In compact mode, both cards are constrained to 420px max width but positioned with `left: 24` and `right: 24`, which means they could overlap when the container is narrow. The first card sits at top: 24 while the second starts at top: 144, so they might not have enough vertical separation to avoid collision depending on the container height.

Looking at the bottom card positioning, it spans the full width from left: 24 to right: 24 with no width constraint, which could cause it to extend beyond the stage area bounds and create layout issues.

Checking the overlay cards more closely, I see the "Physics" and "Readout" cards in Class 11's stage area have maxWidth constraints of 360 and 340 respectively on non-compact layouts. The "Readout" card uses conditional positioning that changes based on the compact flag—appearing at the top when compact is true but positioned differently otherwise.

The real issue appears to be in the Class 6 layout. When examining the StageArea's top card positioning, there's a potential overlap problem if the compact layout's height is constrained. The top card sits at 24 pixels from the top with a maxWidth of 360, and if the observation card positioned at the bottom has insufficient space due to the compact constraint, the two cards could collide.

Looking at the structure more carefully: the control rail takes up flex 6 while the stage area gets flex 5. With typical mobile proportions around 600px height, that gives roughly 273px to the stage. The top card at 24 pixels plus roughly 100 pixels in height leaves only about 173 pixels before the bottom of the screen, which might not accommodate both cards properly depending on how the observation card is sized.

The real problem emerges in compact mode—each overlay card extends across the full width due to `right: 24`, creating a stacking conflict where the observation card at the bottom overlaps with the top card's content area. The observation card should either have constrained width or be positioned below the top card's space rather than competing for the same horizontal strip.

Looking at the working layout in Class 9, the cards maintain proper separation with distinct positioning. Class 6 and 11 both stack cards vertically in the stage area with overlapping bounds, while Class 7's centered approach with `ConstrainedBox(maxWidth: 1180)` avoids this by limiting card widths and centering them within bounds.

The solution involves constraining the bottom "Observation" card in both Class 6 and Class 11 to prevent it from spanning the full width and colliding with the top card.

Now I see the issue. Classes 7 and 9 use a single-column centered layout with `maxWidth: 1180` which adapts well to all screen sizes. Classes 6 and 11 use a sidebar+stage split layout where the overlay cards in the stage area overlap because:

1. **Class 6**: Top card has `maxWidth: 360` but bottom card extends full-width (`left: 24, right: 24`) - they overlap on small screens
2. **Class 11**: Same pattern - the "Physics"/"Readout" cards at top and "Observation" at bottom overlap

Let me fix both:

No errors - only pre-existing warnings. The fixes look good. The key changes I made:

1. **Class 6** (`_StageArea`): Wrapped the bottom "Live Observation" card in `ConstrainedBox(maxWidth: 500)` so it doesn't extend full-width and overlap with the top card.

2. **Class 11** (`_StageArea`): 
   - Removed `right: compact ? 24 : null` from the top-left "Physics" card - now it's left-aligned only
   - Removed `left: compact ? 24 : null` from the top-right "Readout" card - now it's right-aligned only
   - Wrapped the bottom "Observation" card in `ConstrainedBox(maxWidth: compact ? 500 : 600)` to prevent it from overlapping with the top cards
   - Reduced maxWidth of top cards from 420 to 340 for compact mode

These changes ensure that on smaller screens (especially in compact mode):
- The top two cards (Physics + Readout) don't overlap because they each have their own constrained width
- The bottom card doesn't overlap with the top cards because it's also width-constrained

686: class _MirrorPainter extends CustomPainter {
687:   _MirrorPainter(this.result);
688: 
689:   final SphericalMirrorResult result;
690: 
691:   @override
692:   void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
693:     final cy = size.height * 0.62;
694:     final mirrorX = size.width * 0.72;
695:     canvas.drawLine(
696:       Offset(0, cy),
697:       Offset(size.width, cy),
698:       Paint()
699:         ..color = const Color(0xFF94A3B8)
700:         ..strokeWidth = 2,
701:     );
702: 
703:     final mirrorPath = Path();
704:     final mirrorTop = Offset(mirrorX, cy - size.height * 0.33);
705:     final mirrorBottom = Offset(mirrorX, cy + size.height * 0.22);
706:     if (result.type == MirrorType.concave) {
707:       mirrorPath
708:         ..moveTo(mirrorTop.dx, mirrorTop.dy)
709:         ..quadraticBezierTo(
710:           mirrorX + 42,
711:           cy - 12,
712:           mirrorBottom.dx,
713:           mirrorBottom.dy,
714:         );
715:     } else {
716:       mirrorPath
717:         ..moveTo(mirrorTop.dx, mirrorTop.dy)
718:         ..quadraticBezierTo(
719:           mirrorX - 42,
720:           cy - 12,
721:           mirrorBottom.dx,
722:           mirrorBottom.dy,
723:         );
724:     }
725:     canvas.drawPath(
726:       mirrorPath,
727:       Paint()
728:         ..color = const Color(0xFF2563EB)
729:         ..strokeWidth = 4
730:         ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke,
731:     );
732: 
733:     final objectX = (mirrorX - result.objectDistanceCm * 6)
734:         .clamp(30.0, mirrorX - 20)
735:         .toDouble();
736:     final objectBase = Offset(objectX, cy);
737:     final objectTip = Offset(objectX, cy - 80);
738:     _arrow(canvas, objectBase, objectTip, const Color(0xFF059669));
739: 
740:     if (result.imageDistanceCm != null && result.magnification != null) {
741:       final imageX = (mirrorX + result.imageDistanceCm! * 6)
742:           .clamp(20.0, size.width - 20)
743:           .toDouble();
744:       final imageHeight = -80 * result.magnification!;
745:       final imageBase = Offset(imageX, cy);
746:       final imageTip = Offset(imageX, cy - imageHeight);
747:       _arrow(canvas, imageBase, imageTip, const Color(0xFFDC2626));
748:     }
749:   }
750: 
751:   void _arrow(Canvas canvas, Offset base, Offset tip, Color color) {
752:     final paint = Paint()
753:       ..color = color
754:       ..strokeWidth = 3;
755:     canvas.drawLine(base, tip, paint);
756:     final head = tip.dy > base.dy ? -10 : 10;
757:     canvas.drawLine(Offset(tip.dx - 7, tip.dy + head), tip, paint);
758:     canvas.drawLine(Offset(tip.dx + 7, tip.dy + head), tip, paint);
759:   }
760: 
761:   @override
762:   bool shouldRepaint(covariant _MirrorPainter oldDelegate) =>
763:       oldDelegate.result != result;
764: }

996: class _NewtonDiscPainter extends CustomPainter {
997:   @override
998:   void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
999:     final center = Offset(size.width / 2, size.height / 2);
1000:     final radius = size.width / 2;
1001:     final colors = <Color>[
1002:       const Color(0xFFFF0000),
1003:       const Color(0xFFFF7F00),
1004:       const Color(0xFFFFFF00),
1005:       const Color(0xFF00FF00),
1006:       const Color(0xFF0000FF),
1007:       const Color(0xFF4B0082),
1008:       const Color(0xFF9400D3),
1009:     ];
1010: 
1011:     final sweep = (2 * math.pi) / colors.length;
1012:     for (var i = 0; i < colors.length; i++) {
1013:       canvas.drawArc(
1014:         Rect.fromCircle(center: center, radius: radius),
1015:         i * sweep,
1016:         sweep,
1017:         true,
1018:         Paint()..color = colors[i],
1019:       );
1020:     }
1021: 
1022:     canvas.drawCircle(
1023:       center,
1024:       12,
1025:       Paint()
1026:         ..color = const Color(0xFF1E293B)
1027:         ..style = PaintingStyle.fill,
1028:     );
1029:   }
1030: 
1031:   @override
1032:   bool shouldRepaint(covariant CustomPainter oldDelegate) => false;
1033: }

parts.text:
Now I understand the pattern. The Newton's Disc experiment has AI integration with:
1. State variables in the parent: `_aiLoading`, `_aiError`, `_aiQuestion`, `_aiFeedback`, `_aiNextStep`, `_aiOptions`, `_selectedOption`, `_aiIsCorrect`, `_aiBotMood`, `_aiAnswerReview`
2. Two AI methods: `_askAiQuestion()` and `_checkAnswerWithAi()`
3. Default options: `_defaultNewtonOptions`
4. The `_NewtonsDiscExperiment` widget receives all AI-related props and displays the AI tutor UI


5. For plane mirror and spherical mirror, I need to add similar AI state management, create the ask and check answer methods, pass those callbacks down to the experiment widgets, and build out the AI tutor interface components.

**Summary of changes to `class7_lab_screen.dart`:**

1. Added default MCQ options for `plane_mirror` and `spherical_mirror` experiments
2. Added separate AI state variables (`_planeAi*`, `_sphereAi*`) for each experiment
3. Added 4 AI methods: `_askPlaneMirrorAi`, `_checkPlaneMirrorAnswerWithAi`, `_askSphericalMirrorAi`, `_checkSphericalMirrorAnswerWithAi` - each sends relevant student state (object distance, mirror type, focal length, etc.) to the AI backend
4. Updated `_PlaneMirrorExperiment` and `_SphericalMirrorExperiment` widgets to accept AI props and render `_AiTutorCard`
5. Created a reusable `_AiTutorCard` widget with Ask AI / Submit Answer buttons, MCQ options, AI response panel, and bot mood feedback
